summary: First iteration end-to-end test of Church Management System v1.0. All 35 backend pytest cases pass against the public preview URL covering auth (4 roles), RBAC, members CRUD with auto member_id (CHM00001), pastoral notes visibility, families with enrichment, contributions with auto receipt_no (RCP000001) + INR currency, monthly summary, dashboard (admin vs viewer finance gating), users super_admin-only CRUD (incl. cannot-delete-self), audit log, and all 7 report endpoints (PDF + Excel + Content-Disposition). Frontend smoke test as superadmin completed full flow: login -> dashboard (stats + upcoming birthdays) -> members list & search -> add member form -> save -> contributions page -> contribution form -> reports page (6 PDF + 6 Excel buttons) -> users page -> logout -> re-login as viewer (finance hidden, no Users nav). No critical issues.

minor.issue: months dict keys are JSON-serialized as strings (1..12) — tests handle both, but spec sample expected int keys. Cosmetic.

issues: React hydration warning in console: <span> cannot be child of <option>. The warning is triggered by an legacy visual-edit tooling injected <span data-ve-dynamic="true"> wrapper inside <option> elements. Functionality is unaffected, but the warning is noisy in console. Wrap member option text in a constant string template literal or use {`${m.first_name} ${m.last_name}`} directly without intermediate JSX expressions to avoid the wrapper, or ignore as a dev-only artifact.

action_items: No blocking issues. Optional: silence the React hydration warning in ContributionForm.jsx select <option> (caused by legacy visual-edit instrumentation wrapping the text).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~764 lines and mixes models, routes, reports — consider splitting into modules (auth, members, contributions, reports) per the >700 LOC guideline.

CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True which browsers ignore for credentialed requests; current frontend uses bearer tokens so this works, but should be tightened to explicit origin in prod.

create_member endpoint requires staff role but date_of_birth/membership_date/etc are accepted as free-form strings (no ISO validation) — risk of bad data. Consider Pydantic field validators or `datetime.date` typing.

PATCH /members/{id} re-uses MemberIn which requires all required fields — true partial updates aren't supported (clients must send full body). Consider a separate MemberUpdateIn with all-optional fields.
GET /contributions/summary returns months with integer keys but FastAPI/JSON serialises them as strings. Either document this or use string keys explicitly to keep contract stable.
Audit log lacks an index on timestamp; sort+limit on a growing collection will slow down. Add `audit_logs.create_index([('timestamp', -1)])` in startup.
test_credentials: superadmin/Admin@123, admin/Admin@123, staff/Staff@123, viewer/View@123 — all working
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created several TEST_-prefixed members (TEST_John Doe, TEST_Contrib Member, TEST_Fam Head/Spouse) and one contribution (RCP000002, Tithe INR 500 for Jan 2026). One UI-created member 'Frontend UITest<ts>'. These remain in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_cms_backend.py and is the regression source. Test data is prefixed with TEST_ (members + users) and UITest (frontend-created member). Currency is hard-coded INR. JWT bearer token stored in localStorage as 'cms_token'. Roles: super_admin > admin > staff > read_only. Pastoral 'notes' field is stripped for staff/viewer in GET /api/members/{id}. show_finance flag on /api/dashboard/summary controls tithes visibility for viewer.
